intro: The clearest split is that Algolia has a free tier while Meilisearch does not. Algolia is Hosted search API with typo-tolerance, faceting, and relevance tuning for e-commerce and SaaS; Meilisearch is Open-source fast search engine with typo-tolerance, filters, and a simple REST API. The data points Algolia at e-commerce needing sub-50ms search and documentation sites with instant search, and Meilisearch at e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search. Pricing: Algolia is hybrid from $0.50/1K searches (Grow); Meilisearch is hybrid from $30/mo (Build, 50K searches). Lock-in is medium for Algolia and low for Meilisearch, with DX at 5/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 4/5. On listed strengths, Algolia emphasises e-commerce needing sub-50ms search and documentation sites with instant search, and Meilisearch emphasises e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: Algolia is not for budget, simple projects; Meilisearch is not for simple projects, so confirm the fit first, then weigh the pivotal factor above against your budget and lock-in tolerance.
quick_take: Algolia is for e-commerce; Meilisearch is for e-commerce; decide based on whether a free tier matters.
verdict_a: Choose Algolia if you want to start on a free tier before paying and your workload matches e-commerce. Skip it if your context is budget, simple projects.
verdict_b: Choose Meilisearch if starting on a paid subscription upfront is acceptable and your workload matches e-commerce. Skip it if your context is simple projects.

faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $0.50/1K searches (Grow) for Algolia and $30/mo (Build, 50K searches) for Meilisearch. Algolia offers a free tier while Meilisearch does not, so initial cost favors Algolia. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Algolia to Meilisearch?
faq.a: Algolia is tagged with medium lock-in (medium — proprietary api, but instantsearch ui = open-source). Meilisearch is tagged with low lock-in (low — open-source, self-hosted). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Algolia or Meilisearch?
faq.a: Both are rated 5/5 for developer experience in this dataset. With equal scores, the choice comes down to the specific SDK surface and docs you will use day to day. Run a short spike on each before deciding.

faq.q: Is Meilisearch a good alternative to Algolia?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches e-commerce, since both list the same fit. Meilisearch is flagged as not for simple projects, so confirm your context does not fall there. Note that moving from Algolia to Meilisearch means leaving a free tier behind.
